correct_explanation: multiple large matrix-style operators dominate arithmetic work and likely exceed the roofline ridge point
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: memory-bound
REASONING: MinGPT causal attention involves large matrix multiplications (Q×K^T and attn×V) over sequence-length dimensions, along with softmax normalization, which requires multiple passes over the attention matrix. The dominant cost in a typical fused or unfused attention kernel is reading and writing the large attention score matrix (O(n²) in sequence length) from/to global memory, making it memory-bandwidth bound—this is precisely the bottleneck that FlashAttention-style kernels aim to address through tiling and recomputation. Even though the stub kernel here is not implemented, the underlying MinGPT causal attention workload it represents is fundamentally limited by memory traffic for the attention matrix.

Even though the stub kernel here is not implemented, the underlying MinGPT causal attention workload it represents is fundamentally limited by memory traffic for the attention matrix.
